final:
// // This module implements a 1-bit DRAM cell with a data input (D), a word line (WL) to control writing, a bit line (BL) for reading, and an output (Q). The DRAM has an enable signal (EN) to control read/write operations. When EN is high, the DRAM writes D to Q when WL is high; otherwise, when EN is low, Q holds the last value written. When reading, BL reflects the value of Q when EN is low and WL is low.
module dram_cell (
    input wire D,
    input wire WL,
    input wire EN,
    output reg Q,
    output reg BL
);
    always @(*) begin
        if (EN) begin
            if (WL) begin
                Q <= D;  // Write data
            end
        end
        BL <= (EN == 0 && WL == 0) ? Q : 1'bz; // High-Z if reading
    end
endmodule

The DRAM has an enable signal (EN) to control read/write operations. When EN is high, the DRAM writes D to Q when WL is high; otherwise, when EN is low, Q holds the last value written. When reading, BL reflects the value of Q when EN is low and WL is low.
